title: The Season of the Orange Coin
content:
The monk checked the index. Twenty-six. Fear — but no longer extreme. The wound was healing slowly, as wounds do.

All around him, the altcoin pilgrims wept. Their bags, once weightless with promise, had grown heavy with impermanence. The orange coin consumed everything — 58 of every 100 satoshis of faith now flowed into its singular gravity. This was Bitcoin Season. The monk had seen it before.

In Washington, men in suits debated the CLARITY Act. The monk smiled. Clarity, they called it. As if clarity could be legislated. As if the market had ever needed permission to teach its lessons.

Somewhere, a corporation had quietly purchased twenty-two thousand coins while others sold in panic. The monk knew this pattern. The fearful give their keys to the patient. It has always been so.

He watched the Fed's shadow fall over the charts. Powell spoke of inflation, of energy, of patience. The monk nodded. Even central banks practice a form of Zen — though they call it 'data dependency.'

He closed the terminal. The candle burned. The incense rose. Tomorrow the market would open again, indifferent and magnificent.
mantra: In Bitcoin Season, the altcoin monk waits. In waiting, he accumulates wisdom.
